title: Empty statements should be removed
description: Empty statements should be removedAnalyze your codeintentionality-clearmaintainabilityCode SmellQuick FixIDE quick fixes available with SonarQube for IDEunusedWhy is this an issue?Empty statements represented by a semicolon;are statements that do not perform any operation. They are often the result of a typo or a misunderstanding of the language syntax. It is a good practice to remove empty statements since they don’t add value and lead to confusion and errors.Code examplesNoncompliant code examplevoid DoSomething() { ; // Noncompliant - was used as a kind of TODO marker } void DoSomethingElse() { Console.WriteLine("Hello, world!");; // Noncompliant - double ; // ..
recommendation: Remove the problematic code and refactor as needed.

noncompliant: for (int i = 0; i < 3; Console.WriteLine(i), i++); // Noncompliant - empty statement as loop body

logic.detection_conditions: Methods containing empty statements (lone semicolons or double semicolons)
logic.triggering_conditions: Semicolon not following a statement (empty statement) OR double/multiple semicolons
logic.exclusion_conditions: Excludes for(;;) infinite loops, semicolons in strings/comments
logic.never_trigger_on: Infinite loops for(;;), semicolons in strings, semicolons in comments, interface/abstract method declarations
logic.reasoning: Empty statements (lone semicolons) don't perform operations and cause confusion. Pattern detects: standalone semicolons after opening braces, double semicolons, and empty loop bodies (for/while with just semicolon). Excludes for(;;) which is valid infinite loop syntax.
